message.content:
@startuml
skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0

class "Producto" <<A>> {
    id : int
    nombre : String
    stock: boolean
}

class "Pedido" <<C>> {
    direccion_entrega: Direccion
    direccion_facturacion: Direccion
    estado_pedido: String
}

class "Usuario" <<C>> {
    nombre: String
    apellido: String
    edad: int
    direcciones: Direccion[]
    direcciones_facturacion: Direccion[]
    numero_tarjeta: int
    
    void get_direccion()
    
    void set_direccion()
}

class "Direccion" <<C>> {
    pais: String
    municipio: String
    departamento: String
    direccion: String
    observaciones: String
    facturacion: boolean
    correo_electronico: String
}

class "ProductoFisico" <<C>> {
    peso : float
    alto : float
    ancho : float
    largo : float
    coste_transporte : float
}

class "ProductoVirtual" <<C>> {
    numero_licencia: String
}


' Relationships from Producto to subclasses (Generalization)
<|--- Producto
Producto <|-- ProductoFisico
Producto <|-- ProductoVirtual

' Relationship between Pedido and Usuario (Composition - filled diamond on User side implies strong ownership/lifecycle dependency in this context based on visual placement of black diamond near user line end, though standard interpretation might vary slightly without explicit arrowheads showing directionality clearly beyond diamonds). 
User *-- Pedido 

' Aggregation relationships involving Address/Dirección  
Dirección o-- Usuario   
Pedidoo -- Dirección  

Aggregations/Compositions related specifically within Pedidos structure itself? The original shows two lines going outwards towards different entities but both originating at same point which suggests multiple associations or perhaps an error if meant for one entity only; however given clarity issues let's assume separate aggregative links where appropriate per typical e-commerce logic implied by names like 'direccion entrega/factura'. So we'll keep those direct attribute references instead unless more info provided about specific association roles not evident here visually alone . Thus keeping simple attributes inside classes rather than extra connector types since they're already represented through field declarations above !

@enduml
